Harmonized Name:
Ratio of Forced Expiratory Volume in 1 second to Forced Vital Capacity
- UK Variable: FEV1/FVC ratio
- Source Label / Description: The ratio of the volume of air that can be forcibly exhaled in one second (FEV1) to the total volume of air that can be forcibly exhaled (FVC).
- Target Concept: FEV1/FVC ratio
- Standard Vocabulary: LOINC
- Standard Concept Code: 19926-5
- Match Confidence: High
- Summary: The FEV1/FVC ratio is a key measure in diagnosing obstructive and restrictive lung disease. It represents the proportion of a person's vital capacity that they are able to exhale in the first second of forced expiration. The LOINC code 19926-5 is a perfect match for this variable.
